---
title: "Photosynthesis"
output: 
  ioslides_presentation:
    css: custom2.css
    widescreen: true
    smaller: true
---

## Things to Know (#'s)
<hr>
<br/>
<br/>
<br/>

**#Leaf Structure**

<br/>
<br/>
<br/>

**#Light Reaction**

<br/>
<br/>
<br/>

**#Calvin Cycle**


<!-- ## Photosynthesis is complicated.... -->
<!-- <hr> -->
<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- <IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:165px; LEFT:125px; WIDTH:800px; HEIGHT:500px" SRC="images/complicated.png"> -->

## Photosynthesis is the basis for most biomass on Earth
<hr>
<br/>

* **<span style="color:blue">CO~2~ + H~2~0 + light ---> Sugars + O~2~</span>**

<br/>

* **Photosynthesis is an energy pathway**
    + 2 linked reactions
    + light-dependent reaction + Calvin cycle

<br/>

* **Everything takes place in the chloroplast**
    + light reaction = thylakoid membranes
    + Calvin cycle = stroma (liquid portion)

<br/>

* **<span style="color:blue">Light reaction = light energy into chemical energy (ATP & NADPH)</span>**
<br/>
* **<span style="color:blue">Calvin cycle = converts CO~2~ into sugars with chemical energy</span>**


<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:570px; WIDTH:500px; HEIGHT:375px" SRC="images/reactions.jpg">

## Leaf structure is adapted for photosynthesis
<hr>
* **<span style="color:blue">CO~2~ + H~2~0 + light ---> Sugars + O~2~</span>**

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:200px; WIDTH:700px; HEIGHT:500px" SRC="images/leaf_structure2.jpg">

## How stomata work...
<hr>
<br/>
- **<span style="color:darkgreen">CO~2~ enters leaf air spaces and then chloroplasts (organelle) through a concentration gradient</span>**
<br/>
- **<span style="color:darkgreen">Stomata are not always open....WHY??? </span>**

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:270px; LEFT:25px; WIDTH:500px; HEIGHT:400x" SRC="images/stomata4a.jpg">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:270px; LEFT:550px; WIDTH:500px; HEIGHT:400px" SRC="images/stomata4b.jpg">

## Vascular tissues in leaves are confined to veins
<hr>
<br/>

<div style="float: right; width: 65%;">
* **<span style="color:darkgreen">_Transpiration_ (leaf water loss from evaporation)</span> is a by‐product of opened stomatal pores**
<br/>

* **H~2~O transport needs to be sufficient to keep stomata open** 
<br/>

* **Veins represents investment in H~2~O supply (*megaphyll leaves*)**
</div>

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:250px; LEFT:80px; WIDTH:250px; HEIGHT:350px" SRC="images/leafveins.jpg">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:350px; LEFT:395px; WIDTH:600px; HEIGHT:300px" SRC="images/venation.jpg">

## Chloroplasts: Where the photosynthesis takes place
<hr>
<span style="color:blue">**A typical mesophyll cell has 30-100+ chloroplast organelles**</span>

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:190px; LEFT:50px; WIDTH:450px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/chlroplasts4.jpg">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:225px; LEFT:550px; WIDTH:500px; HEIGHT:400px" SRC="images/chlroplasts3.png">

## The nature of light
<hr>
<br/>
<br/>
<br/>

<div style="float: left; width: 45%;">

* **Light is a form of energy, characterized by wavelengths**

<br/>

* **Short wavelengths have more energy than long wavelengths**

<br/>

* **Sunlight includes wavelengths in the visible spectrum,  UV, and infrared**
    + UV is too powerful and infrared to weak for photosynthesis

 </div>

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:190px; LEFT:500px; WIDTH:500px; HEIGHT:500px" SRC="images/light_spectrum.jpg">

## When light hits chlorophyll pigments a few things can happen
<hr>

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:25px; WIDTH:400px; HEIGHT:400px" SRC="images/chlroplasts_light.png">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:475px; WIDTH:600px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/pigments.jpg">

## When pigments absorb photons an electron gets excited
<hr>
<span style="color:blue">**Collections of chlorophyll pigment molecules = *light harvesting complex***</span>

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:190px; LEFT:50px; WIDTH:450px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/excite.jpg">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:190px; LEFT:575px; WIDTH:450px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/resonance.jpg">

## Photosystems (2) convert light energy to chemical energy
<hr>
**<span style="color:darkgreen">Photosystem = packaging of chlorophyll pigments and proteins in thylakoid membranes</span>**

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:190px; LEFT:25px; WIDTH:425px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/photosystem.jpg">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:250px; LEFT:450px; WIDTH:650px; HEIGHT:325px" SRC="images/lightreaction3.png">

## Light reactions: make ATP + NADPH for Calvin cycle
<hr>
<span style="color:blue">**Two photosystems convert light energy into two forms of chemical energy harnessing one electron from splitting water**</span>

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:210px; LEFT:100px; WIDTH:900px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/photosystems2.png">

## 

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:50px; LEFT:125px; WIDTH:800px; HEIGHT:600px" SRC="images/mill.jpg">

## Calvin cycle occurs in the stroma
<hr>
**<span style="color:darkgreen">Calvin cycle = uses the chemical energy stored by the light-dependent reactions to form sugars</span>**

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:250px; LEFT:50px; WIDTH:400px; HEIGHT:300px" SRC="images/calvin3.jpg">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:190px; LEFT:575px; WIDTH:450px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/photosynthesis.png">

## Calvin cycle is a 3-step process
<hr>
<br/>

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:50px; WIDTH:475px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/calvin.jpg">


<div style="float: right; width: 50%;">

* **<span style="color:blue">1. *Fixation*</span>: CO~2~ reacts with 5C molecule (RUBP)**
    + catalyzed by Rubisco enzyme
    + new 6C split into two acids (3C each)

<br/>

* **<span style="color:blue">2. *Reduction*</span>: ATP and NADPH convert 3C acids into 3C sugars**
    + reduction of acids (requires electron carrier)
    + requires energy 

<br/>

* **<span style="color:blue">3. *Regeneration*</span>: Some 3C sugars recycled to make RUBP (5C)**
    + one 3C sugar leaves to make glucose
    + recycling step also needs ATP

 </div>

## Making sugars requires several cycles
<hr>
**<span style="color:darkgreen">Three spins needed to remake RUBP and six spins to make glucose (a 6C molecule) </span>**
<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:190px; LEFT:50px; WIDTH:475px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/calvin2.jpg">

<IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:275px; LEFT:550px; WIDTH:500px; HEIGHT:250px" SRC="images/equation2.png">

<!-- ## Rubisco - the enzyme that dominates carbon fixation -->
<!-- <hr> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- <IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:225px; LEFT:50px; WIDTH:425px; HEIGHT:400px" SRC="images/rubisco.jpg"> -->

<!-- <div style="float: right; width: 55%;"> -->

<!-- * **Most abundant enzyme in the world** -->

<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- * **Most CO~2~ converted into biomass is fixed by Rubisco** -->

<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- * **Large molecule : 16 polypeptides : 8 active sites** -->

<!-- </div> -->

<!-- ## Rubisco: the slow, clunky and cheating carbon fixer -->
<!-- <hr> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- * **Only 3-10 reactions per second** -->

<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- * **20% error rate** -->
<!--     + gets worse at high temperatures -->

<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- * **<span style="color:darkgreen">Photorespiration - Rubisco cheats on CO~2~ with O~2~</span>** -->
<!--     + Rubisco does have a higher affinity for CO~2~ via natural selection -->
<!--     + but.... O~2~ is more prevalent in the atmosphere -->

<!-- <IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:500px; WIDTH:500px; HEIGHT:250px" SRC="images/photorespiration.jpeg"> -->

<!-- ## C3 photosynthesis how did we get here... -->
<!-- <hr> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->
<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- <div style="float: left; width: 55%;"> -->

<!-- * **Evolution doesn't necessarily create the best solution** -->

<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- * **Oxygenation products are completely wasteful** -->

<!-- <br/> -->

<!-- * **2x energy to produce the same amount of sugar than if Rubisco just reacted with CO~2~** -->

<!--  </div> -->

<!-- <IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:675px; WIDTH:325px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/atmosphere.png"> -->

<!-- ## Calvin Benson won a Nobel prize, his TA won best practical joke -->
<!-- <hr> -->

<!-- <IMG STYLE="position:absolute; TOP:200px; LEFT:100px; WIDTH:900px; HEIGHT:450px" SRC="images/bensonwilson.png"> -->
